question.text: In the Silver call option example, if the option premium drops from Rs 3,500 to Rs 3,450 over one month (all else equal), what is the annualized Theta?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Theta = (-50/3500)/1 month = -1.43% per month. Annualized = -1.43% × 12 = -17.14% p.a. **The Trap:** -1.43% is monthly, not annual. -50% overstates decay. -5% undercounts. Always annualize monthly theta by multiplying by 12. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers theta calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A -17% annual time decay means the option loses roughly Rs 50/month or Rs 1.67/day just from time — this cost must be factored into any option buying strategy.
